title: Prunus incana
taxonomy: Prunus incana is classified within the family Rosaceae. This classification is part of the larger order Rosales. It belongs to the genus Prunus, which comprises a wide range of fruit-bearing trees and shrubs. Prunus incana is further divided into different species, each with unique characteristics and features. Through its classification, we can better understand the evolutionary history and relationships within the Prunus genus.

benefits: Prunus incana is a species of cherry tree native to China. Prunus incana has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions. Its bark and leaves contain flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties may contribute to the plant's potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. side_effects: Prunus incana, also known as the Chinese wild plum or Chinese plum, is not well-documented for its use in modern medicine. intro: Prunus incana, also known as the silver buffalo berry or silver buffaloberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include treating various gastrointestinal issues, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Prunus incana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
